The requirements of the OBC with respect to Group A, Division 4 occupancies is intended for occupants gatering in oper air seating areas.  As discussed above, the intent of the design is to maintain the current Group A, Division 4 major occupancy, even though the building will be enclosed at certain seasons.  Therefore, to maintain the same major occupancy classification of A4 for the enclosed bowl area at certain seasons, it is proposed to provide active mechanical ventilation and natural ventilation (smoke control system) for the seating area and egress paths. Seating and egress areas have been designed based on A4 requirements, and since smoke protection is implemented for the enclosed bowl areas, the same A4 occupancy classification can be maintained within seasonal enclosure of the Bowl. In accordance with Article 3.3.2.11 every seat is located so that the travel distance is not more than 45 m measured along the path of travel from the seat to an opening to a passageway leading from the seating area. Majority of these passageways leads to an exit through the west and east concourses. Since the East and West concourses will be mostly open to exterior and partially covered by a roof these spaces are deemed to be an extensions of the Bowl. OBC does not contain requirements pertaining to travel distances within these spaces. Therefore, the analysis of smoke layer and egress paths (including egress time) utilizing computer modeling is proposed. The smoke and egress modeling determined that occupants could leave the building prior to the smoke layer descending and affecting their egress. In accordance with Sentence 3.2.2.11(3)  Exits from outdoor stadia or grandstands shall be located not more than 25 m apart.  The amphitheater is located on an island; therefore, it is not possible to meet the requirement of Sentence (3). Considering proposed measures the proposed design including seasonal enclosure meets the intent of the Code with respect to egress and exits requirements pertaining to outdoor places of assembly occupancy, and as such, the proposed design is acceptable.

Alternative Solution proposal - building will include a seasonal enclosure to the existing tier of seating, (i.e., bowl) that will allow the RBC Amphiteatre to accommodate concerts during winter months. The requirements of the OBC with respect to Group A, Division 4 occupancies is intended for occupants gatering in oper air seating areas.  As discussed above, the intent of the design is to maintain the current Group A, Division 4 major occupancy, even though the building will be enclosed at certain seasons.  Therefore, to maintain the same major occupancy classification of A4 for the enclosed bowl area at certain seasons, it is proposed to provide active mechanical ventilation and natural ventilation (smoke control system) for the seating area and egress paths. Seating and egress areas have been designed based on A4 requirements, and since smoke protection is implemented for the enclosed bowl areas, the same A4 occupancy classification can be maintained within seasonal enclosure of the Bowl. In accordance with Article 3.3.2.11 every seat is located so that the travel distance is not more than 45 m measured along the path of travel from the seat to an opening to a passageway leading from the seating area. Majority of these passageways leads to an exit through the west and east concourses. Since the East and West concourses will be mostly open to exterior and partially covered by a roof these spaces are deemed to be an extensions of the Bowl. OBC does not contain requirements pertaining to travel distances within these spaces. Therefore, the analysis of smoke layer and egress paths (including egress time) utilizing computer modeling is proposed. The smoke and egress modeling determined that occupants could leave the building prior to the smoke layer descending and affecting their egress. In accordance with Sentence 3.2.2.11(3)  Exits from outdoor stadia or grandstands shall be located not more than 25 m apart.  The amphitheater is located on an island; therefore, it is not possible to meet the requirement of Sentence (3). Considering proposed measures the proposed design including seasonal enclosure meets the intent of the Code with respect to egress and exits requirements pertaining to outdoor places of assembly occupancy, and as such, the proposed design is acceptable.

Construct a 48 storey residential building. One level of underground parking. 517 units, approx 30,512 m2 gross floor area. The first of the two development sites is located at the southeast corner of Weston Road and Locust Street (the West Site). The West Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 1175-1181 Weston Road and has a combined site area of approximately 1,143 square metres (0.114 hectares). The second of the two development sites is located further east of the West Site on the south side of Locust Street (the ¿East Site¿). The East Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 7-17 Locust Street and has a combined area of approximately 1,482 square metres (0.148 hectares). The West Site and the East Site are physically separated by a T shaped parcel of land known municipally as 1169 Weston Road that is owned and operated by the Toronto Parking Authority as a surface parking lot (the TPA Lands).

To permit the development of a  21-storey building (Building A on the north-east corner of Lawrence Avenue East and Birchmount Road, an 8-storey mid-rise building (Building B) along the Birchmount Road frontage, a 7-storey mid-rise building (Building C), and a 6-storey mid-rise building (Building D) internal to the site. Additionally, a new public park is proposed along the Dulverton Road frontage having proposed size of 948 square metres. A previous Zoning By-law Amendment Application for the subject property was submitted in 2017, culminating with the Council enactment of By-laws 1137-2018 and 1139-2018 on July 23rd, 2018, permitting the development of 286 residential units in the form of a 21 and seven (7) storey apartment building and three (3) free standing, 3.5 storey townhouse buildings. On July 16, 2021, By-laws 632/633-2021 were approved to amend the original application by increasing the residential unit count from 286 units to 429 units in a similar building massing of apartment and townhouse units. The following refinements/amendments to the currently approved proposed development are as follows: 1. Adjustments to Buildings B, C and D (Building Heights from 7 storeys to 8 storeys for Building B, 3.5 storeys to 7 storeys to Building C and 3.5 storeys to 6 storeys for Building D) of Site-Specific By-laws 1137-2018/1138-2018 and 632-2021/633-2021. 2.To increase the overall number of residential suites from 429 to 488 (to be facilitated via the increased heights to Buildings B, C and D). 3. Modify the overall residential gross floor area from 24,000.00 square metres to approximately 27,000.00 square metres. No changes or reconstruction of any sort is being proposed to Building A.

Site plan application proposes the demolition of the existing gas station located at 2320 Lawrence Ave E. The redevelopment includes the construction of a new gas canopy, a new convenience store/quick-service restaurant with an associated drive-through facility, a patio area, increased parking spaces, cool roofs, bird-friendly glazing, and landscaping throughout the property. The existing car wash will remain.

Proposal includes three buildings. Building "A": A third floor addition to the existing 2-storey building. Building "B": The existing three-storey building (1,387 m2) to remain with interior modifications and renovations only. The first floor will continue to house a synagogue and administrative functions, while the second and third floors will be converted into dormitory accommodations. Building "C": A new three-storey addition that will be constructed and integrated with Building A. The basement level will feature a cafeteria, while the first and second floors will be dedicated to classrooms and offices. The third floor will connect with the newly constructed third floor of Building A to form a dormitory space and additional classrooms.

Construction of a 3-storey building for water treatment process upgrades, including ultraviolet (UV) disinfection and a residuals management facility (RMF). This building, which will typically be unoccupied, has an approximate building area of 2,505 m2 (approximately 71.91m x 35.48m) and a gross floor area of 5,221 m2. There are varying rooftop heights ranging from approximately 8 m to 24 m above grade. There will be 3 new parking spaces available in the drive area. There will also be another single-storey building constructed to house electrical equipment, with an approximate area of 46 m2 (dimensions of 11 m x 4.7 m), along with additional infrastructure work throughout the site. The height of the building is approximately 3.9 m above grade. This building will typically be unoccupied.The project also includes renewable energy infrastructure (solar photovoltaic panels and battery energy storage system, i.e. solar panels) to be installed south of the proposed building area as part of another phase of construction work.

The West Site is comprised of the properties unicipally known as 1175-1181 Weston Road and has a combined site area of approximately 1,143 square metres (0.114 hectares). The second of the two development sites is located further east of the West Site on the south side of Locust Street (the ¿East Site¿). The East Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 7-17 Locust Street and has a combined area of approximately 1,482 square metres (0.148 hectares). The West Site and the East Site are physically separated by a T shaped parcel of land known municipally as 1169 Weston Road that is owned and operated by the Toronto Parking Authority as a surface parking lot (the TPA Lands).

Site Plan Approval application to facilitate the development of Block 4 of the former LCBO lands, part of the comprehensively planned Lower Yonge Precinct.  Blocks 1 and 2 of the LCBO lands are already under development, and Block 3 has been conveyed as a City Park. The proposal is comprised of 3 towers having heights of 70, 78, and 85 storeys atop individual mixed-use podiums.  The north building includes a 990 m² daycare and space for a 5,613 m² elementary public school, both to be located in the existing heritage building on site.  A total of 1,291 m² of non-residential floor area is proposed as well as 173,713 m² of residential GFA, resulting in 2,606 residential units including 178 affordable housing units.

An Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment application in order to redesignate the lands (from Regeneration Areas to Mixed Use Areas and Parks and Open Spaces) for a new GO Station as well as to permit an intensified mixed-use development which would include mid-rise and tall buildings (including 15 towers with heights ranging from 28 to 70-storeys). The redevelopment of this site would be completed in multiple phases with new public and private streets, a new public park (1.25 ha), and two Squares (Station Square (4,311 square metres) and Boulevard Square (3,358 square metres)). This proposal includes a total gross floor area of approximately 701,809 square metres which would be a combination of residential (584,932 square metres), commercial (63,444 square metres), retail (36,364 square metres), and institutional/other (17,071 square metres) uses on the lands municipally known as 2150 & 2194 Lake Shore Boulevard West and 23 Park Lawn Road. This proposal includes a total of 7,504 residential units and 4,422 vehicular parking spaces. Refer to File Number 20 146496 WET 03 SB for the related Draft Plan of Subdivision application in order to establish development blocks and new streets (a total of 10 blocks).

INACTIVE Site Plan Control application to permit the proposed Humber Cultural Hub which is a phased institutional development as part of the Humber College Lakeshore Campus. The proposal includes the partial redevelopment of the existing building with the construction of multiple building additions that would be developed in two phases. The first phase would include the construction of an 8 student residence building that would be connected by 1 to 4-storey academic podiums. The second phase would include a new 3-storey academic podium, with a music and performing arts hall, which would connect to the first phase. The existing 6-storey student residence fronting Lake Shore Boulevard West would remain, as well as the existing 2 and 3-storey library building which would connect to the proposed development. The existing 2-storey academic building component located in the center of the site would be demolished and a portion of the existing surface parking lot would be removed to facilitate the proposal.The total gross floor area of the institutional development would be 49,611 m², of which 30,380 m² would be attributed to the proposed development and 19,231 m² would be attributed to the existing buildings that would be retained on the site.

Site Plan proposal for the first phase of development for the Victory Silos Site North Block, bounded by Lake Shore Boulevard East to the north and the planned extensions of Parliament Street, Queens Quay East and Trinity Street to the west, south and east, respectively. The North Block is approximately 3,690 square metres in size and will be developed with a 41-storey mixed-use building comprised of a 11-storey podium 2 building and a 30-storey tower element located at the west end of the block. The height of the building is 130 metres, excluding the mechanical penthouse. The building has a total gross floor area of approximately 46,316 square metres, including approximately 44,975 square metres of residential gross floor area and approximately 1,341 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, which results in an overall density of 12.55 FSI. The development consists of a total of 691 units: 42 studio units, 339 1-bedroom units, 241 2-bedroom units and 69 3-bedroom units. A total of 227 automobile parking spaces (165 spaces for residents, 44 spaces for visitors and 18 spaces for retail uses) and a total of 704 bicycle parking spaces (633 long-terms spaces and 71 short-term spaces) are provided in the building, as well as a Type G loading space and a Type C loading space. Approximately 804 square metres of interior amenity space and approximately 1,030 square metres of outdoor amenity space is provided. Related subdivision application: 21 233775 STE 10 SB

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes Phase 2 of the TCHC Lawrence Heights revitilization, including new and reconfigured public streets, public parks and community uses, and residential development ranging from 6 to 29 storeys in height. Phase 2 includes the replacement of 213 Phase 2 social housing units, 64 replacement units from Phase 4, and the creation of 180 new TCHC affordable units and 961 market units (total of 1,748 dewlling units).

Application to create new and reconfigured public streets, public parks and 8 new Toronto Community Housing lots, to permit new and replacement residential development in Phase 2 of TCHC Lawrence Heights revitalization.

The Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of three retail commercial buildings with a total gross floor area of 4,071 square metres and a floor space index of 0.36. Access will be provided from York Mills Road leading to a surface parking area with 111 vehicle spaces and 33 bicycle parking spaces. Building A, a one-storey structure, is designed to accommodate either four mid-sized commercial units or a single larger tenant such as a grocery store. Building B is a two-storey structure intended to provide up to seven smaller commercial units on the ground floor with a daycare on the second floor. Building C, a one-storey structure, is designed to accommodate a single tenant, likely a restaurant.

The application proposes to amend the previous development concept to introduce an additional tower, for a total of four, along the northern half of the site, preserving the mid-rise built form fronting  Queens Quay East. The proposed development increases the GFA by 9,230 square metres (m2) (4.8%) to a total of 202,878 m2, comprised of 197,396 m2 of residential GFA and 5,482 m2 of non-residential uses. The application also proposes 45,645 square metres of affordable rental housing GFA (23% of overall site GFA). Approximately 2,850 total residential units are proposed (a 39 unit increase), including 1,129 purpose built rental units and 553 affordable rental units (a 95 unit increase), as well as a 4,100 m2 POPS, a 3,060 m2 community hub providing 62 child care spaces and other community uses, and 2,231 m2 of retail.

Site plan application for Quayside Building 2, a 53 storey, 576 unit building to be delivered as part of the Quayside project. The proposed development includes a total GFA of 40,887 square metres (m2), comprised of 37,636 m2 of residential and 3,251 m2 of non-residential uses. The non-residential use is 3,251 m2 for a Community Hub that will provide a range of community uses, including a 62-child private daycare. A TTC substation is also provided to service the Waterfront East LRT.

Application to create new and reconfigured public streets, public parks and 8 new Toronto Community Housing lots, to permit new and replacement residential development in Phase 2 of TCHC Lawrence Heights revitalization.

City Initiated Rezoning to amend the Zoning By-law for 291 Lake Shore Boulevard East, 2 Small Street and 200 Queens Quay East (Quayside Blocks 1B, 1C, and 2).  A City-owned and operated Community Service, Cultural and Arts Facility is now proposed in the south-easterly portion of Block 1C, fronting on Queens Quay East.  The proposal is to revise the minimum required non-residential gross floor area, including the minimum amount required to be dedicated to Community Services, Cultural and Arts Facilitates and General Institutions; to update Zoning Maps, including Map 3 Base Building Areas and Heights; and to revise Holding and Section 37 provisions.  The proposed Zoning By-law amendment also revises building mechanical height permissions to respond to operational needs of the City¿s selected non-profit housing providers for Quayside.

Proposed new transit station: Park Lawn GO Station.  The station will be accessed from both sides of Park Lawn Road and from both the north and south sides of the Metrolinx-Lake Shore West rail corridor. There are two proposed main entrances to the station located on the east side of Park Lawn Road, one on the north side of the rail corridor and the other on the south side of the rail corridor that will be connected to an adjacent mixed-use development (22 131744 WET 03 SA). A third entrance building is located on the west side of Park Lawn Road.  NOTE: The current documents comprise the second resubmission.

Proposed Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendments for Quayside Master Plan Blocks 3b, 4 and 5. The proposal for Blocks 3b, 4 and 5 includes two residential towers and one mid-rise building, collectively accommodating 8,988 square metres of non-residential Gross Floor Area (GFA) and 119,581 square metres of residential GFA. The residential component includes 1,475 condo units and 407 affordable units. The buildings will flank a portion of a 14,350 square metre east-west "Community Forest" which is continuous through the centre of the broader development. The mid-rise building on Block 3B will also support portion of a rooftop urban farm, which extends the length of the mid-rise buildings on Blocks 1, 2 and 3b connected by a suspended pedestrian bridge across Small Street.  An application is required to amend the City of Toronto Official Plan to permit additional height and density and an application to amend the former City of Toronto Zoning By-law 438-86, as amended by OMB-approved By-law 1174-2010, is required to permit additional height and density, amend the permitted uses, update zoning maps and revise certain zoning standards to permit the proposed development. This application forms part of the broader Quayside Master Plan, which is comprised of seven mixed use buildings on four development blocks and public realm improvements on the interior and perimiter of the site. The development includes 321,760 square metres of GFA allotted as 22,562 square metres of commercial, retail, institutional and other non-residential uses and 299,209 square metres of residential uses supporting 4,634 condo units and 869 affordable housing units. Public realm and infrastructure works to be delivered by Waterfront Toronto include the Queens Quay East extension, plazas at Parliament Street and Queens Quay East, an extended Water's Edge Promenade and a 0.4 hectare park.

Quayside development - Proposed Draft Plan of Subdivision to establish eight blocks, including the Queens Quay East extension, a realigned Parliament Street, three development blocks (Blocks 3, 4 and 5 of the Quayside development), two public roads, a public park (Silo Park), and the Water's Edge Promenade.
